attempts.code_tried:
have hom_sum_dist :
      ∀ {M N : ModuleCat.{u} k} {α : Type} (t : Finset α)
        (f : α → (M ⟶ N)),
        ModuleCat.Hom.hom (∑ i ∈ t, f i) = ∑ i ∈ t, ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f i) := by
    intro M N α t f
    induction t using Finset.cons_induction with
    | empty => simp
    | cons h_notin ih =>
      rw [Finset.sum_cons, ModuleCat.hom_add, Finset.sum_cons, ih]
  simp only [hom_sum_dist]
  sorry

attempts.insight: Following the iter-089 PROGRESS.md mandate's fallback ladder, the prover landed the MINIMUM required (steps a+b) instead of attempting the full (c)–(h) chain inline without LSP. The mandate was correctly framed: structure-only deliveries were forbidden, but a step-by-step partial-progress delivery with the trailing sorry pushed to the deepest committed step is acceptable and preserves the re-scoping signal for the next iter. Mathematical-honesty audit: `hom_sum_dist` has a TRUE signature (distributivity of additive monoid homomorphism over Finset.sum is universally true for ModuleCat.Hom.hom — no false-signature risk).

attempts.insight: Two grounds for rejection per prover task result: (i) partial progress of any non-trivially-failing sub-step would be invisible to the next iter (only `sorry` would remain visible); (ii) the iter-090 prover would lose the 'deepest committed step' signal that iter-089 PROGRESS.md's Forward Scoping uses for re-scoping. Committing the safe minimum (a)+(b) explicitly preserves the iter-090 visibility into where the chain currently stops.
